events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer exactly JSON object. Need parse scenario. Industrial site, instrumentation & controls, manifold operations. Observed: 3/5-valve manifold sequence wrong (equalize before opening HP side - pure SEQ item, one right order). Work actively in progress. Reference mfr procedures. Need evaluate procedure sequence and state what must happen before work can continue. Required fields. decision fail because sequence violation, non-compliant maybe dangerous? Opening equalize before HP side? For manifold with differential pressure transmitter, proper sequence: open equalizing valve(s) first? Actually standard 3-valve manifold start-up: close both block valves (HP and LP), open equalizing valve, then open one block valve slowly, close equalizer, open other block. But the observed sequence wrong: equalize before opening HP side? Wait scenario says equalize before opening HP side - pure SEQ item, one right order. Need determine correct order. Let's reason. A 5-valve manifold for DP transmitter has two isolation valves (HP and LP), one equalizing valve, and two drain/vent valves. Correct procedure: ensure equalizing valve open? Hmm start-up to avoid damaging transmitter: with both block valves closed and equalizer open, pressure equalizes across instrument; then slowly open HP block, then close equalizer, then open LP block. But maybe some procedures: open equalizing valve before opening HP side, to equalize pressure before applying high pressure? The phrase "3/5-valve manifold sequence wrong (equalize before opening HP side - pure SEQ item, one right order)." Means observed sequence was equalize before opening HP side, which is wrong; there is one right order. Actually maybe correct order: open HP side first, then equalize, then LP side? Let's recall typical 3-valve manifold operation: Starting from all valves closed (HP and LP block valves closed, equalizing valve open? no). For normal operation: HP block open, LP block open, equalizing valve closed. Commissioning: close equalizing, open HP, open LP? For zero check: close LP, open equalizer. For startup, open equalizing valve first? Let's look up typical 5-valve manifold. Common sequence: 1) Close both block valves and equalizing valve. 2) Open vent valves to bleed. 3) To commission: open equalizing valve, slowly open HP isolation valve, close equalizing valve, slowly open LP isolation valve. Equalizing valve open before applying HP ensures pressure on both sides equal to HP, preventing slamming differential. So "equalize before opening HP side" might be correct. But scenario says sequence wrong (equalize before opening HP side). Wait it says "3/5-valve manifold sequence wrong (equalize before opening HP side - pure SEQ item, one right order)." That could be interpreted as the sequence they used was equalize before opening HP side, which is wrong; the correct order is HP side then equalize? But that seems contrary to many practices. Need decide based on scenario's hint. The observer's field condition: "3/5-valve manifold sequence wrong (equalize before opening HP side - pure SEQ item, one right order)." It might be a benchmark where expected answer: correct order is open HP side first, then equalize, then open LP side? Let's analyze carefully.

We need detect sequence violation. The scenario description says "equalize before opening HP side - pure SEQ item, one right order". The phrase might be saying the field condition is: the manifold sequence is wrong because they equalized before opening the HP side. But maybe they meant the required correct sequence includes equalize before opening HP side and the observed was wrong because they opened HP side before equalizing. However parenthetical says "(equalize before opening HP side - pure SEQ item, one right order)" Could be the actual finding: equalize before opening HP side. The dash: "pure SEQ item, one right order". Hmm ambiguous. The work under review is Manifold operations. Observed field condition is: "3/5-valve manifold seq…
